name: Platform Staff Role Management
description: Grant/revoke/list Platform Administrator and Super Administrator roles for internal staff, backed by the user_roles table, replacing the single-owner-email/ADMIN_KEY-only path with real multi-user admin accounts.

frontend.evidence: frontend/admin-portal.html:365-369 (grantRole()) and its sibling revokeRole() call CDB_STAFF_AUTH.authFetch against /api/admin/roles/grant and /api/admin/roles/revoke; the page renders a grant/revoke form (:349-359) and a granted-roles table (:256-262), verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/admin-portal.html:272 self-declares 'Internal platform staff tool. Not indexed, not linked from any public page. Access requires a verified staff session.' Independently confirmed not linked from any customer-facing page — a repo-wide search for references to this page across frontend/*.html returns only staff-tool cross-links (frontend/proposal-generator.html, frontend/revenue-command-center.html, frontend/mssp-command-center.html). Non-discoverability from customer nav is by design, not a defect; see the accepted exception recorded in docs/capability-registry/exceptions.json.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/rbac.test.mjs:9-12 imports getPlatformRoles/isSuperAdmin/isPlatformAdmin/can/requireCan/handleGrantRole/handleRevokeRole/handleListRoles directly from workers/src/auth/rbac.js — confirmed by direct import-statement grep, 2026-07-08.

verification.evidence: workers/src/auth/rbac.js and frontend/admin-portal.html read directly; workers/src/index.js:8095-8105 grepped for route registration; workers/test/rbac.test.mjs import statements confirmed.

notes: This is a staff-only internal tool, not a customer-facing capability — customer_journey_complete and the GA vocabulary don't map cleanly onto it (there is no 'customer journey' to browser-verify). No actionable gap was found: backend, frontend, RBAC, and tests all check out for its actual (staff) audience. Priority P7 assigned by elimination (lowest-severity bucket) since none of P1-P6's conditions apply, not because documentation is thin.

name: Role/Plan-Based Frontend Feature Gating
description: Hide, disable, or otherwise adapt dashboard navigation and modules based on the authenticated customer's role and subscription plan, instead of rendering every module to every signed-in user regardless of entitlement.

frontend.evidence: PARTIALLY FIXED 2026-07-09. The sidebar's 17 nav-items still render unconditionally — investigation (backed by workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS, the authoritative per-tier entitlement source) found every tier including FREE has genuine, real, partial access to every one of the 17 pages (e.g. FREE gets 1 API key, not zero; FREE gets ai_analyze, not zero AI access) — so removing/hiding whole nav-items by tier would have been an unevidenced product guess, not a bug fix, and risks hiding functionality a customer genuinely has. Instead fixed the concrete, evidenced case where privilege-based feature availability was actually broken: exportCisoPDF(), syncPlanCards(), and selectPlan() compared the real, uppercase _plan.plan value ('FREE'|'STARTER'|'PRO'|'ENTERPRISE'|'MSSP') against lowercase literals, so their tier-gates silently never fired for any real account (e.g. a paying PRO customer's billing page never showed their plan as 'current', and clicking 'Upgrade to Pro' while already on Pro would re-initiate a new payment flow instead of short-circuiting). Fixed by normalizing the fetched value once per function rather than rewriting every comparison/DOM id. Separately found and fixed: TOOL_CATALOG/PLAN_QUOTA had zero MSSP entries despite MSSP being a real, top-tier backend plan (TIER_LIMITS.MSSP/PLAN_FEATURES.MSSP, both ENTERPRISE-or-better) — MSSP customers saw every tool locked and FREE-tier quota numbers on My Tools; loadKeys() read _plan?.plan?.key_limit (always undefined, since _plan.plan is a string not an object) instead of _plan?.key_limit, always showing a hardcoded '2'. A third case (initAiPage()/submitAiAnalysis()) LOOKED like the same case-sensitivity bug but was deliberately NOT 'fixed' the same way: workers/test/aiBrainEntitlementGate.test.mjs proves POST /api/ai/analyze is intentionally not plan-gated for any tier, so the stale FREE-tier block those functions contained was already-incorrect dead code the case bug was accidentally masking — correcting the case would have reintroduced a real regression (blocking FREE customers from a capability they're contractually, test-verified entitled to). That block and its adjacent fabricated 'queries left' credits counter (read a field, ai_queries_remaining, that GET /api/user/plan never returns) were removed instead. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-12 (start of the 22-paid-feature production-hardening program): the org-role half of this capability, previously flagged below as 'entirely unwired,' is now real and working — frontend/user-dashboard.html:2745-2751 computes canManage=['OWNER','ADMIN'].includes(org.your_role) and isOwner=org.your_role==='OWNER', and uses them to hide (not just disable) the Invite button, Settings card, Audit card, Danger Zone, and per-member role-change/remove controls for anyone without the matching real role — read directly, line-for-line matching the server-side rule in workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js:350-411 (see rbac.evidence below). This was NOT built in this pass; it shipped as part of the Organizations feature (CAP-ORG-001) after this entry's 2026-07-09 last_verified date, and this entry's notes simply weren't updated to reflect that its own stated blocker ("out of scope until organization membership itself has a customer-facing UI") had been resolved.

navigation.evidence: The sidebar itself is the navigation; nav-items are not gated by tier/role (see notes for why), but the pages they lead to now correctly reflect each account's real entitlement where a real, evidenced gate exists.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userDashboardTierGating.test.mjs (2026-07-09, new) — 11 tests reading frontend/user-dashboard.html directly plus cross-checking workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS as the source of truth: confirms the AI-analysis FREE-tier block and its credits counter are gone, confirms exportCisoPDF()/syncPlanCards()/selectPlan() normalize case, confirms loadKeys() reads the real field, confirms every TOOL_CATALOG tiers[] array that includes ENTERPRISE also includes MSSP, confirms PLAN_QUOTA has an MSSP entry, and confirms MSSP's real backend entitlements match or exceed ENTERPRISE's on every PLAN_FEATURES flag. Independently re-verified live via a real headless-Chromium Playwright session (not just static grep): FREE tier confirmed unblocked on AI Analysis with zero JS errors; MSSP tier confirmed showing all 8 tools UNLOCKED and the real '9999/mo' quota (was: 0 tools, FREE quota); PRO tier confirmed showing their billing card correctly marked 'current' with a '✓ Current Plan' button (was: never, for any real account); a FREE-tier regression check confirmed exactly 1 tool (not all 8) shows unlocked, proving the fix didn't become over-permissive. ADDED, re-verified 2026-07-12: workers/test/orgRbacIsolation.test.mjs (11 tests, real node:sqlite-backed handler tests, own verdict: 'org member management is production-grade') proves invite requires OWNER/ADMIN, role changes are OWNER-only, the OWNER cannot be removed, roles are allowlisted, and — checked directly against workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js's actual queries during this pass — the membership lookup is scoped to (org_id, caller's own user_id) on every mutating handler, so a user who is OWNER of a different organization gets no membership row and a 403/404 here, not cross-org access. workers/test/userDashboardOrgManagement.test.mjs, workers/test/orgAuditLog.test.mjs, workers/test/orgScansPagination.test.mjs, and workers/test/phase9OrgDashboardSchema.test.mjs (58 tests total across these 5 files) all re-run clean in this pass.

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html and workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js read directly; workers/test/aiBrainEntitlementGate.test.mjs read to confirm the correct (not-gated) contract for /api/ai/analyze before touching that code path, specifically to avoid 'fixing' a case-sensitivity bug into a real regression. Full backend suite green: 182 files / 1927 tests (181/1916 baseline + 1 new file/11 tests) as of the 2026-07-09 fix. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-12 (start of the 22-paid-feature production-hardening program, item 1 of 22): workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js's handleUpdateMemberRole/handleRemoveMember/handleInviteMember read directly line-by-line to confirm real per-org membership scoping (no BOLA/IDOR — a caller's role is looked up WHERE org_id = ? AND user_id = <their own id>, so authority in one organization never leaks into another); frontend/user-dashboard.html:2745-2751,2765,2770 read directly to confirm the client-side canManage/isOwner gates hide (not just disable) each control under exactly the same rule the server enforces. workers/test/orgRbacIsolation.test.mjs + 4 sibling test files (69 tests total) re-run clean. No code changes were needed — the gap this entry described was already closed by the Organizations feature (CAP-ORG-001), which shipped after this entry's prior last_verified date; only the registry's own record was stale.

notes: Re-scoped from the original finding after evidence-gathering: the original framing ('sidebar should hide items by tier/role') turned out to have no clean, safe implementation — workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's own PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS show every tier has some real, legitimate access to every sidebar page, so wholesale hiding would have been an unauthorized product decision risking real customer-facing regressions (e.g. hiding 'API Keys' for FREE, who do have 1 real key). What genuinely WAS broken — and is now fixed — is that several already-written, already-intended tier-gates inside individual pages never actually fired due to a case-sensitivity bug (comparing the backend's real uppercase tier value against lowercase literals), which affected real paying customers (a PRO customer's own plan never showing as their 'current' plan; MSSP customers, the platform's top tier, seeing every tool locked). UPDATED 2026-07-12: this entry previously stayed P4/PILOT ONLY because org-role-based gating (OWNER/ADMIN/ANALYST/MEMBER/VIEWER) was 'entirely unwired' pending organization membership getting a customer-facing UI. That UI (CAP-ORG-001) shipped in the interim, and with it came real, tested, correctly-scoped org-role enforcement on both the server (workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js) and client (frontend/user-dashboard.html) — this entry's own stated blocker no longer exists, so it now moves to GA APPROVED WITH DOCUMENTED LIMITATIONS. Documented limitation: this re-verification pass relied on the existing unit/integration test suite (69 tests, real node:sqlite-backed handlers) and direct code reading rather than a fresh live-browser session against the org-role UI specifically; the platform's automated CI (Playwright E2E Smoke) covers general site health on every deploy but wasn't run standalone for this one capability in this pass. Priority stays P4 per this file's historical-severity convention (unchanged from when it was assigned, not re-litigated on each re-verification).
